source_intake_record.raw_question_text: e. The electromagnet is composed of layers of spiral wound copper pipe; the pipe has inner diameter $d_{i}=0.40 \mathrm{~cm}$ and outer diameter $d_{o}=0.50 \mathrm{~cm}$. The copper pipe is wound into this flat spiral that has an inner diameter $D_{i}=20 \mathrm{~cm}$ and outer diameter $D_{o}=50 \mathrm{~cm}$. Assuming the pipe almost touches in the spiral winding, determine the length $L$ in one spiral.

image_dependency: All needed quantities (d_i, d_o, D_i, D_o, touching assumption) are explicitly stated in the question text.
Although an image file exists, the registry issues indicate the raw question had only an image placeholder, so image reliance should be scored conservatively low.
multi_step: The problem requires combining multiple geometric quantities: pipe diameter, inner spiral diameter, and outer spiral diameter.
A likely solution involves at least two steps, such as inferring the number of turns from the radial build and then computing total spiral length from the turn diameters/circumferences.
This is a quantitative physics/geometry word problem rather than a direct lookup.
verifiability: The question provides explicit numeric inputs, making the result independently computable.
Multiple-choice options are provided, which supports answer checking.
